turns.student: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
turns.patient: I've had this crampy pain in my lower abdomen, mostly on the left side, and it's been constant for a few weeks now. The diarrhea is what's really got me worried – 8 or 10 times a day, it's like water, with blood sometimes mixed in.

turns.student: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
turns.patient: It's not just uncomfortable, it's debilitating. The fatigue is affecting my work and I've had to cut back on projects at my job because I just don't have the energy.

turns.student: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
turns.patient: I get a bit dizzy and lightheaded when it happens, especially if I haven't eaten much beforehand. And sometimes my heart beats faster than usual, but that's about it. The pain just takes over everything else.

turns.student: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
turns.patient: To be honest, I haven't really noticed much sweating, maybe a bit more than usual when I'm already exhausted from the diarrhea and pain. But the dizziness is definitely something I've experienced, especially if I stand up too quickly after using the bathroom.

turns.student: Thank you for sharing that. I really appreciate your honesty. Does the discomfort stay in one place or does it spread anywhere?
turns.patient: It mostly stays around my lower abdomen, like a steady ache on the left side, but sometimes it radiates to my back, especially when I'm standing up or moving around. The pain is pretty constant, even when I'm not feeling the diarrhea symptoms.

turns.student: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
turns.patient: No, it's mostly just my lower abdomen and back that get affected. The pain is really what's been worrying me, because I don't know what's causing it or how to make it stop.

turns.student: I'm concerned about you. Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Even minor changes?
turns.patient: Honestly, no, I haven't noticed any swelling. My weight has actually been dropping a bit over the past few weeks due to all this diarrhea and not being able to eat much, so that's more of an issue for me right now than swelling anywhere else on my body.

turns.student: Sometimes when people are going through something like this, they also feel nauseous or have stomach upset. Has that happened to you?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've been self-medicating with loperamide (Imodium) for the past 2 weeks because it seemed to help calm down the diarrhea a bit, but it hasn't really done much for the pain or nausea. If anything, I'd say the Imodium sometimes makes me feel worse because it doesn't seem to address the root cause of my symptoms.

turns.student: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
turns.patient: My energy's really low, like I said, it's affecting my work performance, and my appetite is practically non-existent due to the diarrhea. I've been surviving on mostly takeout because cooking feels too much effort when I'm already so exhausted.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
turns.patient: Yes, I've had a low-grade fever, usually around 37.8 to 38.0 degrees Celsius, a few times per week. It's not always there, but when it is, it's just a nagging feeling that I'm not quite right. And to be honest, the nocturnal diarrhea has been really disrupting my sleep - I've been woken up by it 2-3 times a night, which is making me feel even more exhausted and irritable than usual.

turns.student: Your health really matters to me as your care provider. Can you tell me about any other medical conditions you've been diagnosed with?
turns.patient: I had an appendectomy when I was 16, but that was a long time ago and everything seemed to heal up fine after it.

turns.student: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've not been taking anything as prescribed. Like I said earlier, I've been self-medicating with loperamide because it seemed like something was helping, but now I'm not even sure if that's doing any good. The diarrhea is still really bad and the pain is constant. I feel like we're just patching things up instead of figuring out what's causing all this in the first place.

turns.student: I don't want to pry, but understanding your lifestyle really helps me help you. How do you eat on a typical day?
turns.patient: To be honest, it's been pretty irregular due to my work hours. I'm usually grabbing takeout or eating something quick because I don't have time to cook. Sometimes it's just a sandwich or leftovers from last night if I'm lucky. I've not really been following a regular meal schedule like I should.

turns.student: It sounds like your life keeps you really busy. Do you get a chance to exercise or stay active?
turns.patient: I try to, but it's tough with my work hours. I used to go for runs or do some yoga in the mornings, but lately, even that feels like too much effort when I'm already so tired from everything else. To be honest, exercise has been one of the first things to go since all this started happening.

turns.student: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30. That's been on my mind a lot lately, especially since I'm experiencing some similar symptoms.

turns.student: I'm so sorry to hear that. That must have been incredibly difficult. Do you worry about your own health because of it?
turns.patient: Yes, to be honest, it has crossed my mind a lot lately, especially with these symptoms I've been experiencing. The thought of having something like Crohn's disease is really scary for me, and it's made me more anxious about what might be going on with my own health.

turns.student: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
turns.patient: I do drink alcohol, usually 2-3 beers most weeknights, but over the past month, it's increased to 4-5 beers per night because of my stress levels and these symptoms. Drinking has become a way for me to unwind and cope with everything that's been going on.

turns.student: I want to make sure we're being thorough. Do you smoke or use any substances?
turns.patient: No, I don't smoke. And like I said earlier, my drinking has increased a lot lately because of stress and these symptoms, but it's not something I'm proud of – 4-5 beers per night is definitely more than usual for me.

turns.student: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
turns.patient: I appreciate your reassurance. To be honest, I have been feeling a bit guilty about not seeking medical attention sooner and maybe trying to self-medicate with loperamide, but it didn't seem like anyone else was experiencing this kind of symptom, so I thought maybe it was just something I could manage on my own. But now that I'm talking to you, I feel more comfortable opening up and telling you everything, even if it doesn't make me look perfect.

turns.student: Before we wrap up, is there anything else you're worried about that you want to share?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've been really worried about what might be causing these symptoms. The thought of having something serious like Crohn's disease or another condition is scary for me, especially given my sister's experience. I'm also worried about how this will affect my work and personal life in the long term if it doesn't get better soon.
